L1.area: Natural language processing web service request handling and response generation

L2.description: An incorrect access control vulnerability exists where user-supplied HTTP request parameters used to select processing models are reflected in HTTP response headers without validation against the set of known resources. An attacker can supply arbitrary parameter values that bypass the intended access restrictions, potentially manipulating response headers or accessing unintended functionality.

L3.area: Named entity recognition web service and its HTTP request processing logic
L3.description: An incorrect access control vulnerability exists where user-supplied HTTP request parameters used to select named entity recognition models are not validated against the set of loaded models before being used in processing and reflected in HTTP response headers. An attacker can supply arbitrary parameter values, bypassing intended access controls and potentially manipulating response output.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled HTTP request parameters for selecting NER classifier models are not validated against the set of known models, allowing arbitrary values to bypass access controls and be reflected in HTTP response headers.
